name: Arenys de Mar towards Arenys de Munt - circular via the seafront promenade
name_original: Arenys de Mar hacia Arenys de Munt – circular por el paseo marítimo
region_intro: Road cycling around Arenys De Mar provides a varied landscape, combining scenic coastal roads along the Mediterranean Sea with challenging inland climbs. The region transitions from mostly flat coastal stretches to rolling hills and the more demanding terrain of the Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park. This area features a mix of dense forests, cork oak groves, and open views over the Maresme coast. Best road cycling routes around Arenys De Mar The most popular road cycling route is Canet de Mar - Sant Pol de Mar - Arenys de Munt Loop, a 14.9 miles (24.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 15 minutes to complete. This easy route combines coastal sections with a gentle inland loop. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Arenys de Mar towards Arenys de Munt - circular via the seafront promenade, a moderate 13.0 miles (20.9 km) path. This route follows the seafront promenade, offering views of the coast. Local road cyclists also love the Sant Pol de Mar – Canet de Mar Beach loop from Canet de Mar, a 36.0 miles (57.9 km) trail leading through coastal towns and along the beach, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Road cycling around Arenys De Mar is defined by coastal roads, rolling hills, and the natural park's forested climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal loops to challenging mountain passes. The routes in Arenys De Mar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 10,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Arenys De Mar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Arenys De Mar?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes available around Arenys De Mar, offering a diverse range of experiences for all skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 10,000 times.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Arenys De Mar?
A: The region offers a varied landscape, combining scenic coastal roads along the Mediterranean Sea with challenging inland climbs. You'll find mostly flat coastal stretches, rolling hills, and the more demanding forested terrain of the Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park, which features dense forests and cork oak groves.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Arenys De Mar offers several easy road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000912410/canet-de-mar-sant-pol-de-mar-arenys-de-munt-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canet de Mar - Sant Pol de Mar - Arenys de Munt Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 24.2 km trail that combines coastal sections with a gentle inland loop, perfect for a relaxed ride.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/18234722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canet de Mar Beach – Montseny Forest Road loop from Arenys de Mar&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain over 97.4 km. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21225177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordoriol Pass – Revell Pass loop from Arenys de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 114.2 km with over 2,200 meters of ascent.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by notable landmarks and natural features. You might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/132844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Pedra Gentil&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1296433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Ca l'Arenes&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1452213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corredor Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1015284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calella Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views along the coast.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Arenys De Mar?
A: The road cycling routes around Arenys De Mar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from stunning coastal views to challenging inland climbs, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the Arenys De Mar area?
A: Yes, there are several circular road cycling routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995678766/arenys-de-mar-towards-arenys-de-munt-circular-via-the-seafront-promenade" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenys de Mar towards Arenys de Munt - circular via the seafront promenade&lt;/a&gt;, which offers coastal views and a pleasant loop.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Arenys De Mar?
A: The mild climate throughout the year makes the Maresme an ideal destination for road cycling. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures, but even winter can be pleasant for riding due to the region's temperate weather.

Q: Are there specific passes or climbs popular with road cyclists in the region?
A: Yes, the region features rolling hills and more challenging mountain routes with popular passes. Cyclists can tackle climbs to passes like Collsacreu, Parpers, and Sant Bartomeu, which provide significant elevation gain and open views over the inland Maresme.

Q: Is the Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park accessible for road cyclists?
A: The Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park, just inland from Arenys de Mar, offers challenging climbs and varied terrain for road cyclists, including dense forests and cork oak groves. It's a significant draw for those seeking more demanding routes. You can learn more about the park's features on &lt;a href="https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Parc_del_Montnegre_i_el_Corredor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikivoyage&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there options for road cycling routes with low elevation gain?
A: Yes, particularly along the coast. The Maresme coastal route offers scenic, mostly flat stretches along the Mediterranean Sea, allowing cyclists to enjoy breathtaking sea views with minimal climbing. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39551142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pol de Mar – Canet de Mar Beach loop from Canet de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, while moderate in distance, feature relatively low elevation gain for their length.
tips: Very nice road among the vegetation with very few cars.
Beautiful area to see the sea

Canet de Mar is located between Sant Pol and Arenys de Mar.

Apart from tourism, the area also lives from the cultivation of strawberries and flowers.

The historic center has several interesting modernist buildings.
